prompt: Complete using the words in the box: at, in, on, to.
explanation: Verbs of motion (move, go, come, walk, fly) use to. Cities use "arrive in". Exact points such as the end of a street use at. Surfaces use on.
passageTemplate:
1. My aunt and uncle have decided to move {{blank-1}} New Zealand.
2. Do you want to go {{blank-2}} the theatre tomorrow?
3. We stayed {{blank-3}} a great hotel in Dubai.
4. Wait {{blank-4}} the end of the street and I'll come and meet you.
5. You can come {{blank-5}} my house for dinner, if you like.
6. Connor was walking {{blank-6}} the corner shop when he realised he'd lost his wallet.
7. We drove all night and finally arrived {{blank-7}} Lisbon at eight o'clock.
8. Did you leave your book {{blank-8}} the teacher's desk, so she can see it?
9. Look at those sheep {{blank-9}} that field over there.
10. It takes about six hours to fly {{blank-10}} Asia from here.
